artifact_types.label: Objective & Constraints Spec
artifact_types.description: A structured specification of goals, constraints, acceptance criteria, and key trade-offs.

artifact_types.label: Assumptions & Scope
artifact_types.description: Explicit assumptions, scope boundaries, and non-goals; includes what is held fixed.

artifact_types.label: Representation Map
artifact_types.description: A mapping between representations (before/after), including variables, units, correspondences, encodings, and transformation steps.

artifact_types.label: Invariance Claim
artifact_types.description: A strict preservation claim about what properties remain invariant under a method, representation change, or composition step.

artifact_types.label: Tractability Report
artifact_types.description: An analysis of computational/optimization/inference tractability (complexity, conditioning, identifiability) before/after a change.

artifact_types.label: World Model (Runnable)
artifact_types.description: A runnable simulation model or executable system specification, including state, dynamics, and parameters.

artifact_types.label: Dataset / Observations
artifact_types.description: A dataset or observation set used for modeling, calibration, inference, or evaluation.

artifact_types.label: Execution Traces
artifact_types.description: Traces/logs produced by executing a model or procedure (simulation runs, rollouts, traces, seeds).

artifact_types.label: Verification & Validation Report
artifact_types.description: Evidence and results of verification/validation checks (V&V), including what was tested and outcomes.

artifact_types.label: Belief Model
artifact_types.description: A probabilistic belief representation (distributional family, parameters, graphical structure, priors).

artifact_types.label: Uncertainty / Posterior Summary
artifact_types.description: Posterior/predictive summaries, uncertainty intervals, calibration curves, or risk bounds.

artifact_types.label: Forward Model / Simulator Interface
artifact_types.description: A forward model or simulator interface that maps inputs, states, or parameters to observable effects.

artifact_types.label: Inverse Solution
artifact_types.description: An inferred set of parameters/states/causes/structures solving an inverse problem (point/posterior/set-valued).

artifact_types.label: Candidate Solution / Decision
artifact_types.description: A proposed solution/decision (e.g., optimized parameters, chosen policy), with links to objective and constraints.

artifact_types.label: Policy / Ruleset
artifact_types.description: Explicit rules, constraints, or decision policy used to govern execution or enforce invariants.

artifact_types.label: Provenance Log
artifact_types.description: Traceable provenance for artifacts (sources, tools, versions, assumptions, lineage).

artifact_types.label: Operational Tension Belief
artifact_types.description: A structured belief state over operational tensions, including levels and evidence pointers.

artifact_types.label: Preservation Claim
artifact_types.description: A claim about what is preserved, recoverable, bounded, or intentionally altered by a transformation or composite route; invariance claims are a strict subtype.

artifact_types.label: Interface / Composition Contract
artifact_types.description: A specification of interfaces, alignment conditions, handoff semantics, synchronization assumptions, and compatibility constraints when composing stages, models, or paradigms.

artifact_types.label: Surrogate / Emulator Model
artifact_types.description: A reduced-order, statistical, or learned proxy model introduced to emulate a more expensive forward or world model.

design_notes: Artifact types are global and cross-paradigm. They should be coarse enough to remain stable, and fine-grained details should be expressed via artifact subtype/labels, not new global IDs.

Paradigm- or edge-specific documents are expressed as instances of these artifact types plus semantic labels (e.g., 'Calibration report' as a subtype of UM.ART.EVALUATION.VERIFICATION_VALIDATION_REPORT).
Layer-1 artifact families are keyed to route-level functional roles rather than domain file names.
Preservation Claim generalizes Invariance Claim; exact invariance remains available as a narrower specialized artifact family.
Interface / Composition Contract exists because cross-paradigm composition introduces interface burdens not reducible to ordinary assumptions or representation maps.
